event_description: A pickup-trailer pair is tracked across EO and IR while nearby passing cars, roadside poles, and foreground trees create plausible identity swaps.

notes.annotation_visibility_warning: Do not export main_event, event_description, answer, option_roles, or rationales to model prompts.
notes.design_revision_note: Video-reasoning v13: replace broad identity conclusions with specific temporal order, screen-side, occlusion, and EO/IR contrast-transfer judgments.
acceptance_gates: Run prompt leakage audit after request export.
Do not export answer, option_roles, rationale, main_event, event_description, or time_reference metadata to prompts.
Use nearby true-looking alternatives: adjacent vehicles, nearby road edges, neighboring pavement, and modality contrast reversals.
Keep wording natural and answerable by a human viewer from the full video.

qa.question: When the EO view first zooms in on the road segment containing the tow pair and the silver car, what lane arrangement is visible?

options.A: the tow pair is in the upper-right lane, while the silver car is lower-left in the opposite lane
options.B: the tow pair is in the lower-left lane, while the silver car is upper-right in the opposite lane
options.C: the tow pair and the silver car are in the same upper-right lane with little or no separation
options.D: the tow pair is on the road shoulder, while the silver car remains in the adjacent travel lane

qa.question: In the later EO close-up where the truck–trailer pair is seen from the side, where is the roadside pole relative to the pair?

options.A: on the far grass side beside or above the trailer, away from the hitch point
options.B: on the near shoulder beside the truck, not between the truck and trailer
options.C: between the truck and trailer at the hitch point
options.D: beside the rear of the truck, close to but not between the truck and trailer

qa.question: In the close side-on IR view of the truck–trailer pair, which thermal contrast pattern is visible?

options.A: the road edges appear brighter, while the long trailer side looks darker
options.B: the road edges appear darker, while the long trailer side is the brightest region
options.C: the field rows are the brightest features, and the truck–trailer pair is not aligned with the lane
options.D: only two similar warm points are visible, with no elongated side body apparent

qa.question: Across the EO road sequence, in what order do the silver car and the yellow car appear?

options.A: the silver car appears in the first close road pass, and the yellow car appears later in a wider road view
options.B: the yellow car appears in the first close road pass, and the silver car appears later in a wider road view
options.C: the silver car appears first, and the yellow car appears immediately afterward in the same close road pass
options.D: the yellow car appears first in a wide road view, and the silver car appears later near the tow pair

qa.question: As the IR view widens, which visual change indicates that the truck–trailer pair remains on the road rather than shifting into the field?

options.A: the large side-on body shrinks into a smaller body still aligned with the road near the trees
options.B: the large side-on body turns into a square patch inside the field rows
options.C: two compact warm spots beside the road become the tracked object instead of the long body
options.D: the road-edge lines replace the vehicle body as the only visible target

qa.question: In the later EO road view, which dark object is the distractor rather than the tow vehicle?

options.A: dark car near trailer, separated from hitch
options.B: the dark unit directly coupled to the trailer front
options.C: a dark field patch above the road edge
options.D: the pole shadow crossing the far shoulder

qa.question: In the later EO road view, which dark object is a separate distractor rather than the towing vehicle attached to the trailer?

options.A: a dark car near the trailer that is clearly separated from the hitch
options.B: the dark vehicle unit directly coupled to the front of the trailer
options.C: a dark roadside vehicle-like object above the road edge, away from the trailer
options.D: a small dark object on the far shoulder that is not attached to the trailer

qa.question: Which EO-visible detail is unreliable as decisive evidence when matching the target from EO to IR?

options.A: the color contrast between the white trailer and the black truck paint
options.B: the front-rear coupling between the truck and trailer at the hitch
options.C: the elongated trailer body remaining on the lane
options.D: the target motion staying aligned with the road through the view

qa.question: Which temporal detail could lead to a mistaken target swap if it were used without checking the full EO sequence?

options.A: a separate dark car appears near the trailer after the close pass
options.B: the same trailer-like long body remains aligned with the road across views
options.C: the truck–trailer coupling is visible before the later road view
options.D: the target stays on the same road segment as the view widens

qa.question: In the close IR side view, where is the compact unit relative to the long trailer-like body?

options.A: attached to the left end of the long body
options.B: attached to the right end of the long body
options.C: separate above the long body on the field side
options.D: separate below the long body on the near shoulder

qa.question: Which EO-to-IR contrast change best matches the trailer?

options.A: the trailer looks pale in EO, but appears darker than the bright road highlights in IR
options.B: the trailer looks dark in EO, but appears brighter than the road highlights in IR
options.C: the trailer looks pale in EO, and remains the brightest elongated object in IR
options.D: the trailer looks pale in EO, but breaks into only a small compact hot spot in IR

qa.question: When foreground trees partially interrupt the road view, which visible cue best preserves the target identity?

options.A: the elongated road-aligned body, rather than the nearby separate dark car
options.B: the nearby separate dark car, rather than the coupled elongated body
options.C: a dark roadside shadow, rather than either vehicle body
options.D: the field-row texture, rather than the road-aligned object
